name: Esmolol
executiveSummary: Selective beta-blocking agent. Post-coarctation repair hypertension, dyspnea and cyanosis crises, ectopic atrial tachyarrhythmia.
indications:
## Main indications

Selective beta-blocking agent. Post-coarctation repair hypertension, dyspnea and cyanosis crises, ectopic atrial tachyarrhythmia.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Loading 0.5 mg/kg over 1 min, then 50–300 mcg/kg/min.

pediatrico.dose: Loading: 0.5–0.6 mg/kg over 2–4 min, followed by infusion 0.1–0.2 mg/kg/min
pediatrico.notes: Elimination half-life: 9 min. Esmolol may increase serum digoxin and theophylline concentrations. Morphine may increase serum esmolol concentration. The 250 mg/mL ampoule must not be given undiluted; dilute to a final concentration of 10 mg/mL in NS or 5% dextrose.

stability:
## Stability

- Use within 24 h after preparation.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Hypotension, dizziness, fatigue, headache.
